text:
Once upon a time, nestled in a sleepy town known only as Meadowbrook, there lived a ten-year-old girl named Lily. Her summer dreams were as boundless as the deep blue sea. She dreamed of sea adventure, of mermaids and adventures across the seven seas.

One afternoon while playing near Whispering Willow Creek, Lily found an old, weathered map sticking out of an antique book at Mrs. Jenkins’ bookstore. It wasn’t a map to any famous sea—I’m glad—and it had these unusual fish-like creatures sketched all over it. Despite its cracked edges and faded colors, Lily felt compelled to follow its lines.

She followed the map’s directions to an old oak tree, its branches like the arms of some forgotten ocean deity. Beneath the tree, she discovered a rusty key. Picking it up, she heard a tiny voice inside.

“Click.” The key clicked once.

Suddenly, Lily felt dizzy. When she looked down, she saw a shimmering keyhole
